title: How can you implement a custom repository method in Spring Data JPA, and when should this be used?

answers.junior: You can add custom methods to your repository interface for special queries or operations not covered by default methods.
answers.middle: Custom repository methods are used for queries or operations that aren’t supported by default. Implement them in a separate class and link with your repository interface.
answers.senior: Custom methods are implemented in a class with the same name as the repository plus 'Impl'. Use @Repository and inject dependencies as needed. Use for complex queries, batch operations, or business logic.
answers.tricky: Custom implementations can introduce complexity and maintenance challenges. Always document and test custom methods. Advanced use cases may require direct EntityManager access or native queries for performance.
